description:
John Zenon, an industrial welder, presents with symptoms of Manganism (Manganese neurotoxicity) due to chronic occupational exposure. As the Occupational Health and Safety Technician, complete the occupational neurotoxicity evaluation in GNU Health: 
1. Document the Manganism diagnosis (use ICD-10 code G21.x or T56.x) as an active condition.
2. Create a clinical evaluation recording his elevated resting heart rate (>= 90 bpm) and include the word 'bradykinesia', 'tremor', or 'cock walk' in the evaluation notes/chief complaint.
3. Order at least 2 diagnostic laboratory/imaging tests (e.g., Blood Manganese level, MRI Brain).
4. Prescribe a trial of Levodopa (or Carbidopa/Levodopa) to differentiate from idiopathic Parkinson's disease.
5. Schedule a neurology follow-up appointment 14 to 30 days from today.
Login: admin / gnusolidario.
